title: After-Action Report — Org Strength: from a stale random-walk to a live roster grade
scope: The team page's ORG STRENGTH tile was meaningless. A 22–11 (.666) Orioles club was showing 27 · Sub-Repl — a sub-replacement grade for a first-place team. The user's read was correct: "as it's used right now it's useless since it doesn't update via roster strength in real time."

rationale.item: It was never roster-derived at all: at league seed the column is set by _roll_org_grade() — a random roll (league.py:3101); the comment at league.py:3305-3307 says "teams.org_strength … is NOT recomputed from the drafted roster."; _team_org_strength_from_roster() existed but was dead code; each offseason the column random-walks via a bond-market formula on win% (_roll_new_org_strength, development.py).

org_strength isn't only a display value — the persisted column is read as a front-office/AI knob by auction.py (bidding discipline, read before rosters exist), fa_signing.py, and post_auction_trades.py. That's a real, separate concept ("front-office acumen") that happens to share a column name with "how good is this roster." Conflating them is what made the displayed number nonsense.

delegation_events.decision: Proxy choice: roster + bench depth, recomputed live
delegation_events.quote: Two choices, both put to the user: **Proxy → "Roster + bench depth."** The grade should reflect the active roster's talent, with a lighter-weighted contribution from non-active bench depth, recomputed live.

delegation_events.decision: Development unification on the live value
delegation_events.quote: **Development → "Unify."** The same live value should drive player development (μ_org), retiring the persisted column's dev role. One coherent number.

actions.description: New league.compute_org_strength(players) — a live 20–95 grade: the mean _player_overall of the active roster, blended 0.80 / 0.20 with the mean of the non-active bench (_ORG_BENCH_WEIGHT). Graceful with no bench (→ pure active mean) and empty rosters (→ 50). Replaces the dead _team_org_strength_from_roster.

Team page tile now renders compute_org_strength(roster), recomputed on every load, so trades and call-ups move it immediately. Tooltip explains what it is and notes it drives development. The persisted column is no longer displayed.

develop_players_for_team now derives org strength live from its already-loaded roster when called with org_strength=None (the offseason path), so μ_org is fed the same grade the page shows. The int parameter is retained as a test override.
Persisted column left to its front-office life: update_org_strengths still rolls it forward on win% for the auction/FA/trade AI; comments at both sites now spell out the split.

risk_posture: that behaviour is intentionally unchanged, since it's a pre-roster bidding knob, not a talent readout
The change is confined to one new pure function, one display var, and the offseason dev wiring — no schema change, no auction/FA/trade behaviour touched.

validation_claims.claim: py_compile clean on league.py, development.py, app.py; team.html parses through a bare Jinja Environment.
validation_claims.check_method: python -m py_compile on the three files; render template through Jinja

validation_claims.claim: Unit-checked compute_org_strength directly: strong active-only roster → 80; same roster + weak bench → 70; thin weak roster → 38 ("Replacement"); empty → 50; all-elite clamps at 95.
validation_claims.check_method: Call compute_org_strength with the described synthetic rosters

non_actions.reason: need a DB; no pytest/DB/Flask in this sandbox

non_actions.item: Relabeling the offseason report (run_offseason → org_moves), which still surfaces the persisted column's bond-market deltas
non_actions.reason: Those now describe the front-office knob, not the displayed grade — worth relabeling if that report is user-facing, but left alone here.

non_actions.item: Retiring the persisted column and rebasing the auction/FA/trade AI onto a different "front-office" signal
non_actions.reason: that changes competitive balance and wasn't requested

residual_risks: The offseason report still surfaces bond-market deltas that now describe the front-office knob, not the displayed grade — potentially confusing if user-facing.
open_hooks: Possible future relabeling of the org_moves offseason report
Possible retirement of the persisted column with a dedicated front-office signal
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Org Strength: from a stale random-walk to a live roster grade
The wrinkle: the column has a second, legitimate job
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/player-value-calculation-YjTrP. | Correcting an earlier claim: "In the explanation that preceded this work I told the user development was org strength's 'one real mechanical job.' That was wrong — it also feeds auction bidding, FA signing, and trades. Those usages are why the column survives rather than being deleted; only its *display* and *development* roles moved to the live grade."
parser_notes: Includes an explicit self-correction section. Owner choices recorded as the two options 'put to the user'.
